---
title: "Background"
---

This site monitors open-access publishing at the [TU9](https://www.tu9.de/) universities of technology, using [OpenAlex](https://openalex.org/) and snapshotting the figures over time.
The headline metric is the open-access share of each university's corresponding-author output; a few entity-level indicators are kept as context.
Nothing is scraped work by work — the counts come from OpenAlex's server-side aggregation.

## Which institutions

The tracked institutions are the nine universities of the TU9 alliance — one OpenAlex institution each. Affiliated organisations such as university hospitals and research institutes are **not** tracked as separate institutions; where they belong to a university they enter only through the [consolidated view](#consolidated-view-leiden-ranking) below.
The full list, with OpenAlex and [ROR](https://ror.org/) IDs, is in [`data-raw/institutions.csv`](https://github.com/slub/tu9-openalex/blob/main/data-raw/institutions.csv) and can be edited to change the selection.

## Open access on the corresponding-author lens

The denominator is not a university's whole output but the works whose *corresponding author* is affiliated with it, identified through OpenAlex's [`corresponding_institution_ids`](https://developers.openalex.org/api-reference/works#top-level) filter. This corresponding-author lens aligns with the institutional attribution commonly used in [OpenAPC](https://openapc.net/) and transformative-agreement reporting.

The site reports the following corresponding-author figures, identified by the `ca_` prefix:

- **`ca_works`** — works with a corresponding author at the university;
- **`ca_oa_works`** / **`ca_oa_share`** — how many of those works are open access (`is_oa:true`) and the corresponding share;
- **`ca_doaj_works`** / **`ca_doaj_share`** — how many of those works appeared in a journal listed in [DOAJ](https://doaj.org/), using `primary_location.source.is_in_doaj:true`.

These figures are reported by publication year.
Corresponding-author works and OA share are also summarized for the published multi-year period and reference year, while DOAJ figures have a reference-year summary.
For the reference year, the site also reports the mutually exclusive OA-status composition: gold, hybrid, green, bronze, diamond, and closed.

All of this comes from OpenAlex's server-side `group_by` aggregation (per-year and per-status counts), so no individual works are downloaded.

Whether this corresponding-author OA share is a reliable enough basis for routine OA monitoring is exactly the question this dataset is meant to help answer.

## Consolidated view (Leiden Ranking)

Whether a university hospital or an affiliated institute counts as part of "the university" is a judgement call.
The [CWTS Leiden Ranking Open Edition](https://open.leidenranking.com/) makes that judgement explicitly: it links affiliated organisations to a core university as `component` (weight 1, counted fully — e.g. the university hospital), `joint` (fractional), or `associated` (listed but not counted).
The TU9 slice of that mapping is extracted, reproducibly, by `scripts/leiden_affiliations.py` into [`data-raw/leiden_affiliations.csv`](https://github.com/slub/tu9-openalex/blob/main/data-raw/leiden_affiliations.csv) (source: CWTS Leiden Ranking Open Edition 2025 — Data, [Zenodo](https://zenodo.org/records/17471989), CC0; OpenAlex snapshot 08/2025).

So each university appears two ways: as the single OpenAlex institution (identified by its ROR id), and *consolidated* — its OpenAlex id OR-ed with those of its weight-1 `component` affiliates in one `corresponding_institution_ids` filter, so a work still counts once.
The consolidated unit is where, for instance, the [University Hospital Carl Gustav Carus](https://openalex.org/I4210162051) is counted inside TU Dresden and the [Universitätsklinikum Aachen](https://openalex.org/I4210120689) inside RWTH.
Fractional `joint` affiliates are not (yet) included — only full components.
A university without any consolidates to itself, so all nine have a consolidated value and the two views stay comparable across the alliance.

## Core-source-filtered view (CWTS Core sources)

A third view keeps the same Leiden-consolidated member set — the university's OpenAlex institution id OR-ed with its weight-1 `component` affiliates — but additionally restricts works to sources on the CWTS Core sources allow-list using
OpenAlex's `primary_location.source.is_core:true` filter.
This gives corresponding-author works in CWTS Core sources and their open-access share.

The OpenAlex filter combines the corresponding-author lens (`corresponding_institution_ids:<university-and-component-ids>`) with the usual XPAC exclusion (`is_xpac:false`) and `primary_location.source.is_core:true`; the numerator additionally requires `is_oa:true`.
Because the filter is on `primary_location`, a work qualifies when its primary venue is a Core source and not when only a secondary or repository location is associated with one.

This view is meaningful even for universities without Leiden components because the source filter itself narrows the publication universe.
It is populated for all nine TU9 universities.

Important caveat: this is a **source-only Core filter**, not a reproduction of the official Leiden Ranking `core publication` set.
The official CWTS definition of a core publication applies additional publication-level criteria, including language, work type, source type, references, affiliation availability and retraction status.
The Zenodo records below provide the versioned source list and methodology; the live OpenAlex `is_core` flag may evolve after the August 2025 snapshot.

### Provenance

- "Core sources and core publications in OpenAlex", CWTS, 2025. DOI: <https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17200868>. Standalone source list (`source.tsv`) including `source_id`, `source`, `source_type`, `issn_l`, `is_core_source`, `n_works` and `n_core_works`, based on the August 2025 OpenAlex snapshot.
- "A methodology for identifying core sources and core publications in OpenAlex", CWTS, 2025. DOI: <https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17200830>. Methodology report plus the supplementary source spreadsheet (`Supplementary material - Sources.ods`).
- CWTS Leiden Ranking Open Edition 2025 — Data. DOI: <https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17471989>. The `pub.tsv` table includes an `is_core_pub` publication-level field, but the standalone, auditable source list is the separate Core-sources dataset above.

## Context metrics

Beside the OA figures, each snapshot keeps output counts from the Works API and institution entity, together with entity-level citation indicators.
The output counts preserve XPAC-excluded, XPAC-inclusive, and lineage-inclusive lenses for comparison.

- **Output** — total works and works by publication year under the applicable lenses;
- **Citation context** — cited-by count, h-index, i10-index, and 2-year mean citedness.

The fetched OpenAlex institution entity is also archived as JSON per snapshot (`data/snapshots/<slug>/<date>.json`) for source fields not surfaced in the CSVs.

### Why these figures differ from the OpenAlex profile

An institution's page on openalex.org links to its works with `authorships.institutions.lineage:<id>&include_xpac=true`.
That is a wider population than the one counted here, in two independent ways, so the numbers will not match unless both are accounted for. For TU Dresden:

| Lens | Works |
|------|------:|
| `authorships.institutions.id`, XPAC excluded — published here | 129,281 |
| `authorships.institutions.id`, XPAC included — the entity's `works_count` | 139,032 |
| `authorships.institutions.lineage`, XPAC included — what openalex.org links to | 148,325 |

The first step is the Expansion Pack. The second is lineage: OpenAlex's parent/child hierarchy, which rolls a university's child institutions into it.
This dataset counts the institution itself — `corresponding_institution_ids` and `authorships.institutions.id` match one entity and perform no lineage expansion.

The lineage figure is carried in `metrics.csv` as `works_count_lineage_incl_xpac` purely so the two can be reconciled; no metric on this site is derived from it.

Leiden consolidation and OpenAlex lineage define different sets.
They overlap but diverge exactly where it matters: the [University Hospital Carl Gustav Carus](https://openalex.org/I4210162051) is *not* in TU Dresden's OpenAlex lineage (OpenAlex links it as `related`, not `child`), yet Leiden counts it as a `component`.
Conversely an institute such as [NaMLab](https://openalex.org/I4210122489) *is* in the lineage.
This project's consolidated view uses the Leiden `component` mapping, which counts the university hospital.

### Expansion Pack (XPAC) is excluded

In November 2025 OpenAlex added ~190 million works as the [Expansion Pack](https://developers.openalex.org/guides/key-concepts#xpac-expansion-pack) (XPAC) — mainly datasets and repository records with lower average data quality.
Every works query here appends `is_xpac:false`, so `works_count`, the works-per-year counts and all open-access figures exclude XPAC explicitly rather than by relying on OpenAlex's current default.
Because the institution *entity*'s own aggregates include XPAC, `works_count` and works-per-year are taken from the works API (not the entity).
The citation-based indicators (`cited_by_count`, h-index, i10-index, 2-year mean citedness) are the exception: OpenAlex publishes them only as an entity aggregate, so those figures **include** XPAC.

### Entity aggregates lag the live API

The institution entity's own figures — `works_count`, `counts_by_year`, `cited_by_count` and the summary statistics — are precomputed aggregates, while every works query here is answered live. The two are therefore not from the same moment.
For a year that is still filling up this is visible: in July 2026 the live XPAC-*excluded* count of TU Munich's 2026 works (9,497) already exceeded the entity's precomputed XPAC-*inclusive* figure for the same year (9,431).

That is a property of the data, not an error, but it means the two sources should not be compared as though they were simultaneous. The consistency checks in `scripts/validate.R` compare live figures with live figures for exactly this reason.

## How it is assembled

1. For every university in `data-raw/institutions.csv`, the pipeline runs a handful of OpenAlex [works](https://developers.openalex.org/api-reference/works) `group_by` queries — corresponding-author works by year and OA status, total works by year, the Leiden-consolidated corresponding-author view, and the Leiden-consolidated view restricted to CWTS Core sources (`primary_location.source.is_core:true`) — and reads the [institution entity](https://developers.openalex.org/api-reference/institutions) for the citation indicators, using the polite pool and, where configured, a free OpenAlex API key.
2. Everything is fetched into memory first and only then validated: the exact configured institution set, every required product per university, a single consistent snapshot date, and the arithmetic behind each OA share. A coverage guard rail additionally aborts if the total works count drops sharply against the previous snapshot. If this pre-write validation fails, nothing is written.
3. Only after that are the files written: a dated row per university is appended to `data/metrics.csv` (re-running on the same day replaces that day's rows rather than duplicating them), the publication-year and OA views are rewritten for the latest snapshot, and `meta.json` is regenerated. Products that no longer apply are removed rather than left behind as stale files. The stored products are then read back and validated again before they are committed or deployed.

## Caveats

OpenAlex coverage and attribution evolve continuously — affiliation strings are disambiguated over time, and works are added or reassigned.
A change between two snapshots therefore reflects both real publishing activity and improvements in OpenAlex's own data.
Figures are best read as a consistent, reproducible series rather than as an authoritative count.
